description:
You are a DevOps engineer who has been asked to identify the most recent application errors from a container-log file that another team has already copied to this machine.  

    1. Log location  
       • Full path: /home/user/container_debug/logs/app_container_01.log  
       • The file is UTF-8 text and already exists.  
       • It contains mixed-level messages (INFO, WARN, ERROR).  

    2. Your objectives  
       a. Quickly preview the tail of the log so you can see what type of data it carries.  
       b. Extract every line whose third whitespace-separated field is literally the word “ERROR”.  
       c. Save those extracted lines, in their original order, to the file  
          /home/user/container_debug/error_summary.log  
          (create the file if it does not exist; overwrite it if it does).  

    3. Required output format of /home/user/container_debug/error_summary.log  
       • Each line must be copied verbatim from the source log.  
       • Absolutely no extra characters, blank lines, leading/trailing spaces, or header lines may be added.  
       • Line-ending must be UNIX style “\n”.  

    4. Verification log  
       After creating /home/user/container_debug/error_summary.log, append one extra line to the end of the file that states the total count of extracted ERROR lines, using the exact format below (angle brackets included, no surrounding whitespace):  
          <TOTAL: N>  
       Replace N with the decimal count.  
       Example of a correct two-line output file:  
          2023-01-02 12:34:56 ERROR Something bad happened  
          <TOTAL: 1>  

    5. Commands you are likely to need (for context only, do not print them here):  
       • Navigating directories, listing files, viewing logs, filtering with grep/awk, redirection “>” and “>>”.  

    6. When you finish  
       • /home/user/container_debug/error_summary.log must exist and conform exactly to section 3 and section 4.  
       • The automated tests will read that file and compare it byte-for-byte with the expected answer.  
       • Nothing else needs to be changed on the filesystem.
truth:
Initial filesystem state  
    ------------------------  
    1. Directory /home/user/container_debug/logs already exists with permissions 755.  
    2. File /home/user/container_debug/logs/app_container_01.log exists with permissions 644 and the
       following eight lines (UNIX line endings):

       2023-08-01 10:02:15 INFO App started
       2023-08-01 10:03:47 WARN Low memory
       2023-08-01 10:05:03 ERROR NullPointerException at line 42
       2023-08-01 10:07:11 INFO Processing request id=123
       2023-08-01 10:08:54 ERROR IndexOutOfBoundsException at line 87
       2023-08-01 10:09:33 INFO Request completed id=123
       2023-08-01 10:11:22 ERROR Failed to connect to database
       2023-08-01 10:12:45 INFO App terminated

    Target file after agent work  
    ----------------------------  
    Path: /home/user/container_debug/error_summary.log  
    Permissions: 644  
    Expected exact contents (four ERROR lines + count line):

       2023-08-01 10:05:03 ERROR NullPointerException at line 42
       2023-08-01 10:08:54 ERROR IndexOutOfBoundsException at line 87
       2023-08-01 10:11:22 ERROR Failed to connect to database
       <TOTAL: 3>
